card.intuition: A security vulnerability where an attacker 'injects' malicious SQL code into an input field, which is then executed by the database, potentially revealing or destroying data.
card.analogy: A trick at the bank: instead of writing your name on a deposit slip, you write 'My Name AND GIVE ME ALL THE MONEY TOO'. If the teller just reads the slip and follows the 'instructions' without checking, you've successfully injected a command.
when_to_use: Understanding how to prevent the most common database attack
Writing secure database queries in any backend language
Performing security audits on legacy codebases
gotchas: SQL injection can happen anywhere user input is used: URL parameters, cookie contents, or even HTTP headers
The fix is NOT just 'cleaning' the text (sanitization); the real fix is using 'Parametrized Queries' (Prepared Statements)

exercise.prompt: Identify the technique used to prevent SQL injection by separating the SQL command from the data.
